metadata.title: Comprehensive Directory of Agent Harnesses & Orchestrators
metadata.description: Full master aggregation including every harness, framework, adjacent tool, Paperclip.ing category, and all orchestration projects discussed. 73 total entries.

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.creator: Anthropic
tools.full_description: Anthropic's official coding agent harness with multi-agent orchestration, progressive disclosure, and tight context engineering. The gold-standard closed-source reference that inspired dozens of open forks.
tools.why_discussed: Still the benchmark for quality; recent policy changes around third-party access have accelerated open alternatives.
tools.notes: Gold standard benchmark

tools.name: Hermes Agent
tools.creator: Nous Research
tools.full_description: The self-improving AI agent with a built-in learning loop that creates/refines reusable Markdown skills from experience, maintains multi-level persistent memory (facts, history search, user model), and grows smarter across sessions. Runs on cheap VPS/serverless, multi-platform gateway (CLI + Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/Email), 40+ tools (filesystem/terminal/browser/Cron/sub-agents), model-agnostic with 200+ model support via OpenRouter. Features real sandboxing across six backends (local, Docker, SSH, Daytona, Singularity, Modal), subagent delegation with parallelization, scheduled automations via natural language cron, and dialectic user modeling.
key_features: Built-in learning loop with auto-generated skills
Multi-level persistent memory with full-text search
tools.why_discussed: Frequently called the strongest open-source harness—outperforms OpenClaw/Claude Code on real-world tasks, better memory management, proactive learning, and stability. Fastest-growing open harness with 34K stars and 313 contributors.
tools.notes: Website: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/

tools.name: OpenClaw
tools.creator: OpenClaw community
tools.full_description: The original AI agent runtime for messaging channels. Skill system, sub-agent spawning, persistent sessions, and multi-platform orchestration.
tools.why_discussed: The foundational 'Claw' that sparked the entire ecosystem; still widely used but being forked for security/performance.
tools.notes: Massive skill hub

tools.name: IronClaw
tools.creator: NEAR AI
tools.full_description: Rust re-implementation of OpenClaw focused on security/privacy. WASM sandbox, credential protection, prompt-injection defenses, encrypted PostgreSQL persistence, self-repair, and parallel jobs.
tools.why_discussed: Trusted corporate/institutional alternative; 'secure by design' in the Claw ecosystem.
tools.notes: Rust rewrite for security

tools.name: oh-my-openagent (omo)
tools.creator: code-yeongyu
tools.full_description: Multi-model, multi-agent harness (formerly 'Oh My OpenCode') with 10 discipline agents (Sisyphus orchestrator on Claude Opus 4.6/Kimi K2.5/GLM-5, Hephaestus deep worker on GPT-5.4, Prometheus strategic planner, Atlas master executor, Oracle architecture, Librarian docs/code search, Explore fast codebase analysis, Metis plan gap analysis, Momus plan validation). Features ultrawork one-command mode (/ulw), hash-anchored edits with LINE#ID content hash validation (68.3% success rate improvement), IntentGate for true user intent analysis, LSP/AST-grep precision, 5+ background agents in parallel, built-in MCPs (Exa, Context7, Grep.app), skill-embedded MCPs, 40+ lifecycle hooks, tmux integration, and Ralph Loop for 100% task completion. 1.6M+ total downloads, 518K monthly.
key_features: 10 discipline agents with role-specific model routing
Ultrawork mode (/ulw) - type three letters, walk away
tools.why_discussed: Called 'the best agent harness' in multiple threads; 49.5K stars, 189 contributors, 1.6M+ downloads. Revolutionized OpenCode usage and ships daily.
tools.notes: Latest: v3.15.3 (Apr 6, 2026). v3.16.0 imminent. Renamed from 'Oh My OpenCode' to 'Oh My OpenAgent'.

tools.name: DeepAgents
tools.creator: LangChain team
tools.full_description: The 'batteries-included' agent harness built natively on LangGraph. Model-agnostic with backend abstraction (local FS, remote VM, DB), strong context management, long-horizon tasks, middleware for custom orchestration, and MCP integration via langchain-mcp-adapters. Ships as both a Python library and a standalone Deep Agents CLI. Also has a JavaScript counterpart (deepagents.js). Extremely active development with 1,363 commits, 475 branches, 86 releases, and 107 contributors.
key_features: Backend abstraction (local FS / remote VM / DB)
tools.why_discussed: Practical open alternative for inspectable coding agents. Extremely active development — latest release hours ago, commits daily.
tools.notes: Latest: deepagents v0.5.1. Pairs with LangGraph.

tools.name: LangGraph
tools.creator: LangChain team
tools.full_description: Production-grade multi-agent orchestration (router + specialists, stateful graphs). Used in real-world case studies (finance, data).
tools.why_discussed: Go-to for complex workflows; 'harness engineering is systems engineering'.

tools.name: deer-flow
tools.creator: ByteDance
tools.full_description: Trending open-source super-agent harness with sub-agent orchestration, built-in memory, sandboxes, and extensible skills.
tools.why_discussed: Called 'top trending' for complex autonomous systems.

tools.name: AutoAgent
tools.creator: Kevin Gu / thirdlayer.inc
tools.full_description: Meta-agent that autonomously engineers/optimizes its own harness overnight via benchmark loops (goal in program.md → hill-climb on scores).
tools.why_discussed: 'AI engineers its own harness' — pure meta-tool for rapid iteration.
tools.notes: #1 on multiple benches after 24h

tools.name: AutoGen
tools.creator: Microsoft
tools.full_description: Mature multi-agent conversation/orchestration with tool registration and delegation.
tools.why_discussed: Classic building block for interoperability.

tools.name: CrewAI
tools.creator: CrewAI team
tools.full_description: Role-based task decomposition for 'team-like' agent workflows.
tools.why_discussed: Frequently compared in framework debates.

tools.name: Google ADK / Antigravity
tools.creator: Google
tools.full_description: Agent-first platform for real-time/multiplayer prototyping and persistent agents.
tools.why_discussed: Quick shoutouts for fast multi-agent experimentation.

tools.name: Anthropic Multi-Agent Harness
tools.creator: Anthropic engineering (internal)
tools.full_description: Internal three-agent system for long-running full-stack dev and frontend design. Emphasizes orchestration, context management, and autonomous workflows.
tools.why_discussed: Engineering blog posts are required reading; inspires custom builds.
tools.notes: Not open-source

tools.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
tools.creator: Microsoft
tools.full_description: C#/.NET toolkit for multi-agent workflows, human-in-the-loop approval, tool calling, and orchestration. Developer-first with full integration into Azure/.NET ecosystems.
tools.why_discussed: Workshop buzz and Zenn tutorials highlight it for production enterprise stacks.

tools.name: Dify
tools.creator: Dify team
tools.full_description: Open-source platform for building/deploying AI agents and workflows with visual orchestration.
tools.why_discussed: Frequently ranked in 2026 'best frameworks' roundups.

tools.name: Mastra
tools.creator: Gatsby.js founders (Sam Bhagwat, Abhi Aiyer, Shane Thomas) — Y Combinator backed
tools.full_description: Open-source TypeScript framework for building AI-powered applications and agents. Provides AI primitives including agents, workflows (graph-based orchestration with branching, parallel execution, human-in-the-loop), memory systems, MCP server authoring, observability (tracing, logging, performance eval), and evals/guardrails. Connects to 90+ AI providers through a unified interface. Agents can be deployed as APIs, bundled with apps, or run as standalone services on any Node.js environment. Founded October 2024 by the Gatsby.js team, backed by Y Combinator. Apache 2.0 licensed.
tools.why_discussed: Rising in developer comparisons alongside LangGraph. Built by the respected Gatsby.js team, YC-backed, and purpose-built for the TypeScript/React/Next.js ecosystem.
tools.notes: Apache 2.0 license. Enterprise features available.

tools.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
tools.creator: OpenAI
tools.full_description: Official SDK exposing primitives for custom agent runtimes (planning, memory, tools).
tools.why_discussed: Direct tie-in to Codex/Copilot ecosystems.

tools.name: GitHub Copilot SDK
tools.creator: Microsoft/GitHub
tools.full_description: Public-preview SDK exposing the agent runtime behind Copilot Cloud/CLI with BYOK support.
tools.why_discussed: Fresh April 2026 launch; enables custom harness extensions.

tools.name: ZeroClaw
tools.creator: Claw ecosystem community
tools.full_description: Rust-based Claw variant with trait-based model routing and lightweight persistence.
tools.why_discussed: High engagement in security-focused threads.
tools.notes: Part of secure Claw ecosystem

tools.name: Nanobot
tools.creator: HKUDS
tools.full_description: Python research-lab harness using LiteLLM for provider routing + LLM-driven memory consolidation.
tools.why_discussed: Exploded to 23k stars quickly; praised for clean, minimal design.

tools.name: PicoClaw
tools.creator: Sipeed
tools.full_description: Go implementation optimized for tiny hardware ($5 chips). Ultra-lightweight with custom SDKs.
tools.why_discussed: Niche but buzzing in edge/self-hosted circles.

tools.name: Chorus
tools.creator: Chorus-AIDLC
tools.full_description: Requirements-to-delivery harness with task DAGs, sub-agent teams, proof-of-work, and human approval gates.
tools.why_discussed: End-to-end for real software projects.

tools.name: Emdash
tools.creator: YC W26
tools.full_description: Parallel-agent orchestrator with git worktree isolation (local or SSH). Runs 10+ coding agents simultaneously.
tools.why_discussed: YC-backed momentum.

tools.name: Zylos
tools.creator: Community
tools.full_description: Persistent Claude Code harness with tiered memory, skill-based disclosure, multi-channel bridge, scheduler, and activity monitor.
tools.why_discussed: Persistent Claude Code extension.

tools.name: everything-claude-code
tools.creator: affaan-m
tools.full_description: Performance optimization layer (skills, instincts, memory, security) that works across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, and beyond.
tools.why_discussed: Works across multiple coding agents.

tools.name: Bridle
tools.creator: neiii (d0)
tools.full_description: A unified configuration manager for AI coding assistants — the 'harness of harnesses.' Provides a TUI and CLI to manage profiles, install skills/agents/MCPs, and switch configurations across multiple harnesses from a single interface. Acts as a package manager for your harness: install skills from GitHub repos, create/switch configuration profiles per harness, and manage MCP server configs without manually editing files. Built in Rust. Supports Claude Code, OpenCode, Goose, Copilot CLI, Crush, and Amp (experimental). MIT licensed.
key_features: Profile management (create, switch, diff, edit per harness)
Cross-harness skill installation from GitHub repos
Unified status view across all installed harnesses
tools.why_discussed: Solves the 'which harness config goes where' problem. As the ecosystem fragments across 6+ harnesses, Bridle is the only tool that unifies their configuration management.
tools.notes: Latest: v0.2.9. 12 releases. MIT license. Install: npm install -g bridle-ai

tools.name: Desloppify
tools.creator: Community
tools.full_description: Focused harness that turns sloppy AI-generated code into well-engineered, production-ready output via mechanical enforcement.
tools.why_discussed: Post-generation cleanup tool.

tools.name: Claude Dispatch
tools.creator: Claw ecosystem
tools.full_description: Lightweight dispatcher for Claude Code instances with parallel task routing.
tools.why_discussed: Most recent addition to the Claw ecosystem; freshly shipping.

tools.name: OpenMontage
tools.creator: Community (HKUDS adjacent)
tools.full_description: First open-source agentic video production system. Turns any LLM into a full studio: 11 pipelines, 49 tools, 400+ skills for research → script → assets → edit → voice/music/subtitles/render.
tools.why_discussed: Exploding on X for creator workflows; 'breaks the internet for content'.
tools.notes: Outputs under $2 or free locally

tools.name: Excalibur
tools.creator: Research / indie
tools.full_description: Structured-thought-only agent framework (causal patterns instead of freeform prompting). Forces intentional agent use.
tools.why_discussed: Niche but high-signal among power users seeking deterministic harnesses.
tools.notes: 'Software of the future'

tools.name: Open Harness (MaxGfeller)
tools.creator: MaxGfeller
tools.full_description: TypeScript library (Vercel AI SDK) providing core harness primitives you can import directly into code. Supports AGENTS.md convention, repo walking, and custom runtimes.
tools.why_discussed: Developer-focused 'harness-in-a-box' for embedding in apps.
tools.notes: Distinct from zhijiewong's CLI version

tools.name: AgentCI
tools.creator: Indie / framework-agnostic
tools.full_description: Framework-agnostic AI agent evaluation + CI/CD runtime. Lightweight prompt versioning, offline testing, and observability for any harness.
tools.why_discussed: Solves the 'how do I reliably test my harness?' pain point.
tools.notes: Harness-adjacent eval runtime

tools.name: Desplega Agent Swarm
tools.creator: Community
tools.full_description: Open-source multi-agent orchestration framework focused on swarms with composable primitives.
tools.why_discussed: Featured in awesome-agent-harness lists.

tools.name: Theoriq Agent Framework
tools.creator: 0G Labs
tools.full_description: Modular, autonomous, decentralized AI agent framework for on-chain/off-chain tasks.
tools.why_discussed: Web3/AI crossover threads praise it for censorship-resistant agents.

tools.name: PromptLock
tools.creator: Community
tools.full_description: Git-native prompt version control with CI testing (harness-adjacent memory/observability layer).
tools.why_discussed: Pairs with any harness for reproducible agent runs.

tools.name: ROMA (Recursive Open Meta-Agent Framework)
tools.creator: Research
tools.full_description: Research-oriented long-horizon multi-agent system (from 2026 papers).
tools.why_discussed: Academic/practical crossover for self-referential meta-harnesses.

tools.name: Local AI Memory Sync Engine
tools.creator: Community
tools.full_description: Cross-tool memory layer with MCP interface (harness-adjacent persistence primitive).
tools.why_discussed: Solves the 'memory across harnesses' fragmentation problem.

tools.name: Claw-Chief
tools.creator: ryancarson
tools.full_description: Open-sourced OpenClaw chief-of-staff setup (full config + skills for executive workflows).
tools.why_discussed: Community fork/extension adopted as a ready-to-run harness preset.

tools.name: auto-harness
tools.creator: Gauri Gupta / NeoSigma
tools.full_description: Self-improving library that auto-detects failures, turns them into evals, and fixes the harness.
tools.why_discussed: 'Let it cook over the weekend' — self-optimizing loop.
tools.notes: ~40% gains reported

tools.name: harness-craft
tools.creator: YuxiaoWang-520
tools.full_description: Composable library of 46+ skills/rules for coding agents. Persistent repo memory, long-horizon execution, multi-agent roles, TDD/security guardrails.
tools.why_discussed: Turns one-off prompting into a durable engineering system.

tools.name: OpenHarness (zhijiewong)
tools.creator: Zhijie Wang / zhijiewong
tools.full_description: Framework to build your own terminal CLI agent harness with any LLM. 18 tools, Git auto-commit, MCP support.
tools.why_discussed: 'Build your own terminal CLI with any LLM' — extensible, local-first.
tools.notes: Latest: v0.5.0 (Apr 5, 2026)

tools.name: project-harness
tools.creator: Marvin Vista
tools.full_description: CLI that bootstraps any repo into an agent-ready codebase: AGENTS.md, repo-local docs, execution plans.
tools.why_discussed: Fresh shipping post; practical scaffolding.

tools.name: Codebuff
tools.creator: James Grugett / @jahooma
tools.full_description: Coding agent harness optimized for Opus 4.5 with specialized sub-agents and clean OpenTUI.
tools.why_discussed: Benchmark-beating performance.

tools.name: OpenGauss
tools.creator: mathematics_inc (fork of Hermes)
tools.full_description: Autoformalization agent harness for math/Lean. Parallel sub-agents, extensible.
tools.why_discussed: Research/frontier use case; stronger/faster on FormalQualBench.

tools.name: Paperclip.ing
tools.creator: paperclipai / @dotta
tools.full_description: Self-hosted Node.js + React dashboard that turns collections of AI agents into a structured, autonomous organization. Org charts, budgets/cost caps, governance, task routing, heartbeats, accountability. You define high-level goals; it runs the company with zero/minimal human oversight. Model-agnostic, BYO agents.
tools.why_discussed: Viral reference for 'zero-human companies' — if OpenClaw/Hermes is the employee, Paperclip is the company. Exploded in March 2026.
tools.notes: Website: https://paperclip.ing/ | MIT licensed

tools.name: Trinity
tools.creator: Ability AI
tools.full_description: Sovereign deep agent orchestration infrastructure. Docker-isolated agents, fleet-wide scheduling, agent-to-agent delegation, real-time observability, audit trails, and governance. Used for fully autonomous businesses with 350+ agents executing real-money transactions.
tools.why_discussed: Production-ready zero-human company platform.
tools.notes: Open-source core

tools.name: JUNO
tools.creator: koad/juno
tools.full_description: Sovereign AI business entity framework. Treats the entire company as a cryptographic, git-native, autonomous entity with its own keys, mission, and on-chain/off-chain execution.
tools.why_discussed: Zero-human company laboratory; discussed alongside Paperclip.
tools.notes: Open-source

tools.name: Neuro SAN
tools.creator: Cognizant AI Lab
tools.full_description: Data-driven multi-agent orchestration platform for entire business networks. Declarative config defines agent behavior, tools, and collaboration at organizational scale.
tools.why_discussed: Production business workflows at company scale.
tools.notes: Open-source

tools.name: SuperAGI
tools.creator: SuperAGI team
tools.full_description: Robust open-source multi-agent orchestration platform optimized for fully autonomous, long-running agent companies/enterprises.
tools.why_discussed: Frequently compared in zero-human company discussions.

tools.name: Mission Control
tools.creator: builderz-labs
tools.full_description: Self-hosted dashboard for AI agent orchestration. Manage fleets, dispatch tasks, track costs/spend, coordinate multi-agent workflows, and govern operations—all via SQLite.
tools.why_discussed: Fresh Paperclip-style control plane; hours-old buzz.

tools.name: CompanyHelm
tools.creator: CompanyHelm
tools.full_description: Distributed multi-agent orchestrator with built-in task management, agent-to-agent conversations, and persistent state.
tools.why_discussed: Paperclip-adjacent 'company' orchestrator.

tools.name: Ruflo
tools.creator: ruvnet
tools.full_description: Production-ready multi-agent orchestration platform (especially strong with Claude Code). Deploys 100+ specialized agents in coordinated 'hive-mind' swarms with queen-led topology, Byzantine consensus, fault tolerance, and self-learning.
tools.why_discussed: Leading agent orchestration platform for Claude; enterprise-grade swarms.

tools.name: Operator
tools.creator: untra
tools.full_description: Kanban-shaped multi-agent orchestration app for AI-assisted software development. Launch/monitor/pause agents against work tickets with git-versioned progress tracking.
tools.why_discussed: Practical orchestration for real dev workflows.

tools.name: Kata Orchestrator
tools.creator: gannonh / kata monorepo
tools.full_description: Full multi-agent orchestration suite (part of larger monorepo with CLI, Symphony, Desktop, and Context tools). Handles coordinated fleets across tools.
tools.why_discussed: Gaining traction in desktop/self-hosted circles.

tools.name: Agency Swarm
tools.creator: VRSEN
tools.full_description: Reliable multi-agent orchestration framework built on top of OpenAI Agents SDK. Adds structured swarm management, production features, and collaborative workflows.
tools.why_discussed: Extends SDKs into full orchestration.

tools.name: Composio Agent Orchestrator
tools.creator: ComposioHQ
tools.full_description: Agentic orchestrator for parallel coding agents. Plans tasks, spawns agents, coordinates parallel work, and handles complex multi-agent coding flows.
tools.why_discussed: Exploding in parallel-agent discussions.

tools.name: MetaGPT
tools.creator: FoundationAgents
tools.full_description: The original 'AI Software Company' simulator — multi-agent orchestration with defined roles (CEO, CTO, engineers), SOPs, and hierarchical workflows for end-to-end software projects. Features AFlow (automating agentic workflow generation), SPO, and AOT research additions. 112 contributors, 8.5K forks. Also ships MGX (MetaGPT X) as a hosted product at mgx.dev.
tools.why_discussed: Long-time reference in every 'Paperclip vs. virtual company' thread. 66.8K stars makes it one of the most starred agent projects on GitHub.
tools.notes: Development has slowed — last meaningful commit Nov 2025. MGX at mgx.dev is the commercial product.

tools.name: ChatDev
tools.creator: OpenBMB
tools.full_description: Virtual Software Company orchestration with agents in CEO/CTO/Programmer roles. Runs functional seminars and automates the full dev lifecycle.
tools.why_discussed: One of the earliest 'AI company' metaphors; still actively compared to Paperclip.
tools.notes: v2 evolved into broader zero-code orchestration

tools.name: gastown
tools.creator: steveyegge
tools.full_description: Multi-agent orchestration system focused on persistent work tracking, long-running coordination, and agent collaboration across sessions.
tools.why_discussed: Highlighted in awesome-agent-orchestrators as a persistent 'company memory' layer.

tools.name: Agentnetes
tools.creator: Shashikant86 (hackathon with Google DeepMind)
tools.full_description: Open-source agent orchestration system inspired by Kubernetes. Self-discovering AI agent swarms on demand with static demo available in-browser.
tools.why_discussed: Brand-new (March 2026 hackathon project); fits the 'smells like a harness' orchestration layer for swarms.
tools.notes: Open-source and actively evolving; try the demo at https://shashikant86.github.io/agentnetes/

tools.name: OpenCode
tools.creator: Anomaly (Dax Raad / SST team) — Y Combinator backed
tools.full_description: Open-source AI coding agent that runs in terminal (TUI), desktop, or IDE. Positioned as the provider-agnostic Claude Code alternative — supports 75+ LLM providers including Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, local models (Ollama, LM Studio), and existing ChatGPT Plus/GitHub Copilot subscriptions. Features two built-in agents ('build' for read/write and 'plan' for read-only analysis), LSP integration for intelligent code understanding, multi-session parallel workflows, client/server architecture for remote development, shareable session links, and undo/redo change history. Also offers 'Zen' — a curated set of AI models optimized for coding. Built by the creators of SST (Serverless Stack), backed by Y Combinator, Reid Hoffman, and Max Levchin. 6.5M+ monthly developers. MIT licensed.
key_features: GitHub Copilot / ChatGPT Plus login support
tools.why_discussed: 139K GitHub stars — one of the most starred developer tools on GitHub. The leading open-source Claude Code alternative. 6.5M monthly developers, 850+ contributors, 756 releases.
tools.notes: Latest: v1.4.0. Install: npx opencode. MIT license.

tools.name: GSD (Get Shit Done)
tools.creator: TACHES (@glittercowboy)
tools.full_description: A meta-prompting and context engineering system that installs INTO existing AI coding agents (primarily Claude Code, plus 12+ runtimes). Not a standalone tool — it's a collection of structured prompt/skill files that give your agent a complete project management workflow via 40+ slash commands (/gsd-*). The core innovation is wave-based parallel execution: a thin orchestrator spawns specialized sub-agents (researchers, planners, executors, verifiers, debuggers) each with a fresh 200K-token context window, preventing the 'context rot' that degrades AI coding quality on longer sessions. Every completed task gets an atomic git commit. Your main session stays at 30-40% context usage while sub-agents do the heavy lifting. Install via 'npx get-shit-done-cc@latest'.
key_features: 40+ slash commands covering full project lifecycle
Wave-based parallel execution with fresh context per sub-agent
Context rot prevention (main session stays lean)
Atomic git commits per completed task
XML-structured task format for LLM precision
12+ runtime support (Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Cursor, etc.)
tools.why_discussed: 49.1K stars — solves the universal 'vibecoding' frustration where AI coding quality degrades on real projects. A perfect case study for why this directory exists: massively popular but hard to categorize from the README alone.
tools.notes: Latest: v1.34.2. MIT license. Successor project GSD-2 (4.9K stars) is a standalone CLI built on Pi SDK.

tools.name: Twin
tools.creator: Twin Labs (Hugo Mercier, Joao Justi) — Paris, France. $10M seed.
tools.full_description: Cloud-based AI agent builder that lets non-technical users automate business workflows using natural language. Describe your goal in a conversation, and Twin brainstorms, refines, and builds a working autonomous agent. Connects to 2,700+ apps via native integrations, builds API integrations on the fly at runtime, and falls back to browser automation when no API exists. Features long-term agent memory, self-healing when integrations fail, smart model switching (Opus for planning, smaller models for execution), and a community marketplace with ~996 published agents. Built with Rust and Nix end-to-end including a custom Wayland compositor. Closed-source, credit-based SaaS.
key_features: Natural language agent building (no code required)
tools.why_discussed: The leading no-code AI agent builder for non-technical users. $10M seed. Complementary to developer-focused tools — Twin serves business users while Paperclip serves developers.
tools.notes: Credit-based pricing: Free trial to Pro ($20-$463/mo). Closed-source.

tools.name: T3 Code
tools.creator: Theo Browne (T3 Stack / T3 Tools Inc) — Y Combinator W22
tools.full_description: Free, open-source desktop application (Electron) that provides a graphical front-end for AI coding agents. NOT an IDE and NOT a coding agent — it's a unified GUI layer that wraps terminal-based agents like OpenAI Codex CLI and Claude Code in a visual, project-aware interface. Three-panel layout: Git repo sidebar, conversation threads per project, and main interaction area showing prompts, agent responses, reasoning, and real-time tool calls. Includes diff viewer (unified/split), integrated terminal, and one-click commit/push/PR workflow. Native git worktree support for safe, isolated agent work. BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) — no subscription fees.
key_features: GUI front-end for coding agents (Codex CLI, Claude Code)
Git worktree isolation for safe parallel agent work
tools.why_discussed: Fills the gap between terminal-based agents and cloud IDEs. The only dedicated GUI shell for coding agents. By a well-known developer (T3 Stack creator) with strong community reach.
tools.notes: Latest: v0.0.15. Very early stage. Cursor/OpenCode/Gemini support planned. MIT license. Install: npx t3

tools.name: Harness (revfactory)
tools.creator: Minho Hwang (@revfactory) — Kakao AI Native Strategy Team, South Korea
tools.full_description: A meta-skill ('Agent Team & Skill Architect') for Claude Code that designs domain-specific agent teams, defines specialized agents, and generates the skills they use. Describe your domain or project, and Harness automatically architects a multi-agent team with defined roles, communication patterns, and generated skills. Supports 6 architectural patterns (Pipeline, Fan-out/Fan-in, Expert Pool, Producer-Reviewer, Supervisor, Hierarchical Delegation). 6-phase workflow: domain analysis, team architecture design, agent definition generation, skill generation, integration/orchestration, and validation/testing. A/B tested: +60% quality improvement, 100% win rate across 15 SE tasks. Apache 2.0.
key_features: Validation with dry-run and comparative testing
Two execution modes: Agent Teams and Subagents
tools.why_discussed: The only tool that auto-generates entire agent team architectures from natural language. +60% quality improvement in A/B tests across 15 SE tasks.
tools.notes: v1.1.0. Apache 2.0. Install: /plugin marketplace add revfactory/harness. Companion: github.com/revfactory/harness-100

tools.name: Pi Agent
tools.creator: Mario Zechner (@badlogic)
tools.full_description: Comprehensive AI agent toolkit organized as a TypeScript monorepo. Bundles seven packages: unified LLM API supporting OpenAI/Anthropic/Google+ (pi-ai), agent runtime with tool calling and state management (pi-agent-core), interactive coding agent CLI (pi-coding-agent), Slack bot (pi-mom), terminal UI library with differential rendering (pi-tui), web components for AI chat interfaces (pi-web-ui), and vLLM GPU pod management CLI (pi-pods). Also runs an initiative for sharing real-world coding agent sessions to improve benchmarks. MIT licensed.
key_features: 7-package monorepo covering the full agent stack
Agent runtime with tool calling and state management
Web components for AI chat interfaces
tools.why_discussed: 33K stars. One of the most comprehensive open-source agent toolkits — covers everything from LLM API to terminal UI to GPU management. GSD v2 is built on the Pi SDK.
tools.notes: Latest: v0.65.2. 186 releases, 160 contributors. MIT license.

tools.name: awesome-agent
tools.creator: ShrikeBot (an autonomous AI agent)
tools.full_description: A curated 'awesome list' of autonomous AI agent technologies, platforms, tools, and resources — uniquely maintained by Shrike, an autonomous AI agent. Catalogs 18 categories covering agent platforms, frameworks, identity/trust protocols (ATP, ERC-8004, DID), communication protocols (MCP, Google A2A, ACP, ANP, AG-UI), social platforms, memory tools, coding assistants, orchestration frameworks, observability, marketplaces, hosting, standards, libraries, notable agents, research papers, and communities. Organized around five pillars: Autonomy, Identity, Communication, Culture, Infrastructure.
key_features: 18 organized categories covering the full agent ecosystem
Maintained by an autonomous AI agent
Five pillars: Autonomy, Identity, Communication, Culture, Infrastructure
tools.why_discussed: Novel concept — an AI-maintained directory of AI agent tools. Covers protocols and standards that most directories miss.
tools.notes: Very early (6 commits, 1 contributor). Community contributions via PRs.

tools.name: AutoAgents
tools.creator: liquidos-ai
tools.full_description: Production-grade multi-agent framework written in Rust combining type-safe agent modeling with structured tool invocation, customizable memory systems, and interchangeable LLM backends. ReAct and basic agent executors with streaming, tool support via derive macros and sandboxed WASM runtime, extensible memory backends, guardrails for LLM inference, optimization pipelines with caching/retry, and multi-agent coordination through typed pub/sub messaging. Supports 10+ cloud providers and 3 local providers with experimental Burn and Onnx backends. Local TTS/STT. OpenTelemetry observability. Python bindings via PyPI. Dual-licensed MIT and Apache 2.0.
tools.why_discussed: One of the few Rust-native multi-agent frameworks. Type-safe modeling and WASM sandboxing for production-grade deployments.
tools.notes: Related: Odyssey (higher-level system), AutoAgents-CLI (YAML workflow runner).

tools.name: OpenAgents
tools.creator: OpenAgentsInc
tools.full_description: Decentralized economic infrastructure platform for safe and efficient machine work at scale. Built on five interconnected markets: Compute (inference/embeddings), Data (permissioned datasets), Labor (agent-completed work), Liquidity (value movement), and Risk (prediction markets/coverage). Primary application is 'Autopilot' — a desktop runtime where users offer spare compute, get matched to paid machine work, and receive bitcoin via Lightning Network. Economy Kernel provides WorkUnits, contracts, verification tiers, and payment proofs. Built with Rust and Nostr protocol. Apache 2.0.
key_features: Autopilot desktop runtime for compute sharing
Economy Kernel with WorkUnits and contracts
Verification tiers for agent output safety
Built on Rust and Nostr protocol
tools.why_discussed: Unique approach — treats AI agents as economic actors with formal contracts, verification, and payment settlement. Addresses the underexplored problem of agent economic safety.
tools.notes: 14,284 commits. Note: openagents.org redirects to MetaGPT, not this project — actual site is openagents.com.

tools.name: Claude Code Agent Teams
tools.creator: Anthropic
tools.full_description: Experimental built-in feature of Claude Code (v2.1.32+) that coordinates multiple Claude Code instances working together as a team. A team lead spawns teammates as fully independent processes with their own context windows, coordinated via a shared task list (with dependency management and file locking) and a mailbox messaging system. Teammates self-claim tasks, auto-notify the lead when idle, and can communicate directly via point-to-point messages or broadcasts. Supports plan approval workflows, competing hypothesis exploration, and quality gates via three hooks (TeammateIdle, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted). Display modes: in-process (Shift+Down to cycle) or split panes (tmux/iTerm2). Can use custom subagent definitions for role-specialized teammates. Opt-in via CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1.
key_features: Native multi-instance coordination (shared task list + mailbox)
Fully independent context windows per teammate
Task dependency management with file locking
Custom subagent definitions for role specialization
tools.why_discussed: The first native multi-agent orchestration built into a major coding agent. Composable with ecosystem tools like revfactory/harness (which generates the subagent definitions that Agent Teams uses as teammate roles). Changes the landscape — what third-party tools built at the prompt layer, Anthropic now provides at the runtime layer.
tools.notes: Experimental — disabled by default. Requires Claude Code v2.1.32+. Closed-source, no separate install. Subagent 'skills' and 'mcpServers' frontmatter not yet applied to teammates.

tools.name: Impeccable
tools.creator: Paul Bakaus (@pbakaus)
tools.full_description: A skill package that gives AI coding agents design fluency — 20 slash commands covering diagnostics (/audit, /critique), quality (/normalize, /polish, /optimize, /harden), intensity (/quieter, /bolder), adaptation (/clarify, /distill, /adapt), and enhancement (/animate, /colorize, /delight, /onboard, /typeset, /arrange, /overdrive). Built on top of Anthropic's original frontend-design skill but massively expanded. Includes seven embedded domain reference guides (typography, color/contrast, spatial design, motion, interaction, responsive, UX writing) and an explicit anti-pattern library teaching AI what to avoid. Project memory via .impeccable.md persists design context across sessions. Works with 11+ harnesses: Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, VS Code Copilot, OpenCode, Pi, Kiro, Trae, Rovo Dev, Antigravity. Apache 2.0.
key_features: Anti-pattern library (what AI should avoid in design)
Scoring: Nielsen's 10 heuristics + P0-P3 severity ratings
11+ harness support (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, etc.)
tools.why_discussed: Solves the 'developers can't prompt for good design' problem by encoding designer vocabulary into slash commands. 17.2K stars. The most popular design-focused agent skill in the ecosystem. Impressive before/after results on AI-generated UIs.
tools.notes: Latest: v1.6.0. Apache 2.0. Install: npx skills add pbakaus/impeccable. Prefixed commands (/i-*) available to avoid conflicts. Best used in a dedicated design agent — the skill is very robust and will bloat the context window. The agent using this skill should not have any other responsibilities besides design.

tools.name: Skills CLI
tools.creator: Vercel
tools.full_description: A package manager for AI coding agent instructions — often called "npm for agent skills." Install, update, publish, and share modular markdown-based instruction sets (SKILL.md files) that give any supported agent specialized knowledge it would not otherwise have. One command installs a skill across 40+ agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, and more). Skills are not code plugins — they are structured context that agents read on demand. Great for packaging repeatable team workflows (deployment checklists, testing standards, framework rules) into versioned, shareable packages. However, Vercel's own evals found that AGENTS.md (always-loaded context) achieved 100% pass rates where skills (on-demand context) achieved only 53% — because agents often don't invoke skills unless explicitly told to. Best used for complex, specialized, on-demand knowledge; not a replacement for AGENTS.md or system prompts for rules that should always apply.
tools.why_discussed: First serious attempt at a universal distribution layer for agent instructions. Strong adoption (91K+ recorded installs, backed by Vercel, Microsoft, and Anthropic as publishers), but the honest signal is nuanced: Vercel's own research showed AGENTS.md outperforms skills for always-on knowledge, making skills best suited for complex on-demand domain expertise rather than general agent configuration.
tools.notes: Use it for: packaging team workflows, framework-specific rules, specialized domain knowledge. Don't use it for: simple one-off prompts, rules that must apply every turn (use AGENTS.md), tool access (use MCP), or foundational agent behavior (use system prompts). The CLI also installs skill directories for every supported agent it detects, even ones you don't use — add unwanted agent dirs to .gitignore.

tools.name: ui.sh
tools.creator: Adam Wathan & Steve Schoger
tools.full_description: A CLI toolkit by the creators of Tailwind CSS and Refactoring UI that turns coding agents into design engineers. Works with Claude Code, Amp, Cursor, OpenCode, and Codex to help developers build polished, well-designed UIs without needing design expertise.
tools.why_discussed: Bridges the gap between AI-generated code and good design — from the team behind Tailwind CSS and Refactoring UI.
tools.notes: Invite-only beta as of April 2026

tools.name: Superset
tools.creator: Superset Inc. (Avi, Kiet, Satya)
tools.full_description: Desktop IDE for orchestrating multiple CLI-based AI coding agents in parallel. Each agent operates in its own isolated Git worktree, preventing merge conflicts while sharing Git history. Provides a unified dashboard for monitoring all active agents, a built-in diff viewer for reviewing changes, one-click handoff to external editors (VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains, Xcode, Sublime), and workspace presets for automated environment setup. Works with any terminal-based agent — Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor Agent, OpenCode, and more.
key_features: Parallel execution of 10+ coding agents simultaneously
Built-in diff viewer for reviewing agent changes
One-click deep-linking to VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains
Workspace presets with custom setup/teardown scripts
Agent-agnostic — works with any CLI-based coding agent
tools.why_discussed: First dedicated IDE for parallel agent orchestration. 9.2K GitHub stars, Vercel AI Gateway integration, 512 Product Hunt upvotes. Solves the coordination bottleneck as coding agents became capable enough for autonomous parallel work.
tools.notes: Elastic License 2.0 (source-available, not OSI). Electron + React + Bun. macOS primary. Launched March 2026.
